message:
refactor: extract drain_bash_output() with selective cancellation semantics

Extract the inline bash output draining loop from run_bash_command() into a
dedicated async function drain_bash_output() to improve testability and
enable fine-grained cancellation control.

Key behavioral change:
- New allow_cancellation parameter controls whether the drain loop respects
  cancellation signals. When false (post-exit or post-timeout), the drain
  runs to deadline ignoring cancellation to ensure all buffered output is
  captured. When true (process still active), cancellation is honored.
- This prevents the race condition where output produced between process
  exit and drain completion could be lost due to a concurrent cancel signal.

New tests:
- test_drain_bash_output_ignores_cancellation_after_process_exit(): verifies
  post-exit drain captures all output despite active cancellation
- test_drain_bash_output_honors_cancellation_while_process_still_active():
  verifies active-phase drain correctly respects cancellation

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
